parsed_review.summary: CP00-620 is a descriptor-only Data Room/VDR pack (RP20.P03.M06.S13-RP20.P04.M03.S20, risk C, 150 units) bridging P03 API/interface tail into P04 UI rows. Read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the implementation is complete and internally consistent across registry.js, service.js, validators.js, the contract JSON projections, the validator script, model.test.js, and the README CP00-620 entry. The CP620 binding, section rows, micro-titles, and deliverable distribution match the baseline manifest and prompt exactly; I independently re-derived all 150 row classifications and every deliverable count ties out (implementation 53, ui 30, security_audit 16, contract 17, test 13, claude_review 11, hermes_evidence 7, fixture 3), so coverage validation is substantive rather than vacuous (the base result populates every field the validator asserts). No-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced in depth: the freeze chain sets all dispatches_*/executes_*/writes_*/reads_* guards false, the CP620 validator asserts ~40 such conditions including UI runtime guards, and the no_write_attestation is mirrored in the contract and asserted by tests. Authority boundary is preserved everywhere: claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true; Hermes is used only for the deterministic descriptor evidence packet/receipt (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false) and is not embedded in product runtime. The pack makes no overclaim: manifest production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor, and the closeout artifacts (command-evidence, claude-review-result, construction-inspection, adjudication) all honestly report pending. No P0/P1/P2 issues. One P3 informational note: production_ready closeout remains gated on recorded passing validation-command evidence, the Hermes H20 gate, adjudication finalization, and human final approval — process steps outside this read-only review's authority, not code defects. My review is independent and read-only; it is not final approval and does not assert enterprise trust.

findings.title: Production-ready closeout still gated on un-recorded validation runs, Hermes gate, and human approval
findings.details: command-evidence.json reports status=pending with an empty commands array (line 9) and hermes_gate=pending; claude-review-result.json, construction-inspection.json, and adjudication.md are likewise pending; manifest.json reports production_ready=false and runtime_ready=false. The CP620 descriptor implementation, contract projections, tests, validator script, and no-write/no-real-data boundaries are complete and internally consistent (verified by read-only inspection plus independent deliverable-count math totalling 150). A read-only review can confirm the code is structured to satisfy the expected validation commands (npm test, npm run validate, closeout-pack:validate, etc.) but cannot confirm those commands were actually executed and passed, because no command evidence is recorded yet.
findings.recommendation: Before flipping production_ready to true, record passing evidence for the listed validation commands in command-evidence.json, complete the Hermes H20 gate, finalize adjudication, and obtain human final approval. Keep the pack descriptor-only with runtime_ready=false until a downstream pack explicitly opens runtime behavior under human sign-off; do not weaken the production_ready gate on the basis of this read-only review.
findings.post_review_disposition: Expected pre-closeout process state, not a code defect. Does not block the review verdict (PASS_WITH_FINDINGS) and does not block pack closeout from the reviewer's side; it documents the residual mechanical/human gates the closeout tooling must still enforce.
